title: asclepias ochroleuca
intro: Asclepias ochroleuca, also known as the yellow milkweed or green antelopehorn milkweed, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. As a member of the milkweed family, it has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate milk production in lactating women and its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's roots and leaves are believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, calming the nervous system, and treating digestive issues. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Asclepias ochroleuca are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions.
